title: SeedSmith: LLM-Driven Seed Synthesis for Directed Fuzzing
snippet: Directed fuzzing steers fuzzers toward user-defined sink functions to identify vulnerabilities, but it frequently fails to trigger crashes even after long campaigns. title: AI in the Classroom: A Multi-University Study on Student Learning Outcomes
snippet: A collaborative study involving several R1 research universities examines the impact of generative AI tutors on student learning outcomes. The findings suggest that while AI tools improve initial comprehension, they can reduce long-term retention if not paired with active learning strategies.
match_reason: websearch (publication) — This is a major study on AI use in higher education teaching and learning, directly relevant to the committee's academic focus.

title: GaP: A Graph-as-Policy Multi-Agent Self-Learning Harness For Variational Automation Tasks
snippet: For robots to work reliably in commercial and industrial applications, can recent advances in agentic coding systems combine interpretable robot programming with the open-world adaptability of model-free policies? We introduce Graph-as-Policy (GaP), a multi-agent coding harness that generates directed computation graphs with perception, planning, and control nodes.
match_reason: websearch (publication) — Ken Goldberg is a co-author of this newly published robotics and AI paper on arXiv.

title: RoboVista: Evaluating Vision Language Models for Diverse Robot Applications
snippet: Diverse applications for robotics, such as industry and agriculture, require robots to operate across various embodiments, changing visual conditions, and complex planning. Vision-Language Models (VLMs) offer a promising foundation for general-purpose and interpretable robotic reasoning. title: SeedSmith: LLM-Driven Seed Synthesis for Directed Fuzzing
snippet: Directed fuzzing steers fuzzers toward user-defined sink functions to identify vulnerabilities, but it frequently fails to trigger crashes even after long campaigns. title: An Exploratory Study of Interpretation Difficulty in Cybercrime-Related Discord Chats
snippet: This paper presents an exploratory study of interpretation difficulty in Discord chats related to cybercrime. We construct reference interpretations of purposefully selected difficult messages, which were reviewed by an expert. We then use them to evaluate human and large language model (LLM) interpretations under different context conditions.
match_reason: websearch (publication) — Giovanni Vigna is a co-author of this newly published research paper evaluating human and LLM interpretations of cybercrime chats.

title: California State University Systemwide AI Strategy Focuses on Preventing a Digital Divide
snippet: The California State University system highlights its ongoing systemwide AI strategy aimed at providing equitable access to AI tools for its 470,000 students. The initiative treats technology access as a core equity issue to prevent a new digital divide among public university students.
match_reason: websearch (op_ed) — Directly addresses AI access, equity, and systemwide policy within California's public higher education ecosystem.

title: Middle States Commission on Higher Education Launches New Use of Artificial Intelligence Policy
snippet: The Middle States Commission on Higher Education (MSCHE) officially launched its new 'Use of Artificial Intelligence Policy' alongside its fifteenth edition of Standards for Accreditation, establishing formal regulatory expectations for member institutions.
match_reason: websearch (position_statement) — Involves institutional governance, policy, and accreditation standards for AI in higher education.
